attacking_style: Pure sprinter who relies entirely on leadout train execution in final 250m. Does not initiate attacks; dependent on being launched at optimal moment (150-200m) for finishing kick.
team_tactics: Requires elite leadout train (Deceuninck-QuickStep with Morkov) to control final kilometers and deliver to front. When leadout fails or is disrupted, shows inability to self-position through chaos.
terrain_preference: Standard flat sprint stages with wide finishes. Exposed by technical/narrow finales (Champs-Élysées) and climbs within 15km of finish (Côte de Meron). Cannot handle positioning battles through technical sections.
vulnerability: Positional awareness deficient on technical finishes; loses wheels when leadout disrupted. Poor timing judgment when launching own sprint (waits too long, ~150m). Vulnerable to late climbs shredding trains before finale.
key_patterns: Repeatedly loses position on technical finishes despite strong leadout support (Champs-Élysées, Cofidis chaos)
Sprint timing inconsistent: launched too early (200m, faded) or too late (150m, missed advantage)
Vulnerable to disruption 15-75km from finish: climbs and accelerations expose positioning weakness
Performed domestique duty on climbs for Alaphilippe despite being pure sprinter, suggesting team role flexibility
form_trajectory: negative: 2020 Paris-Nice showed continued positioning/timing problems even after team change to Cofidis, indicating persistent tactical deficiencies rather than team-specific issues
